description.en: This board states the lab objective. The walkthrough follows the same order students use to verify Kirchhoff's Current Law.
Use these controls to check wiring, record readings, plot the graph, reset the setup, print results, or auto-connect the circuit.
For a guided run, add the default circuit wires first, then press CHECK to validate and lock the connections.
After the connections are verified, set R1, R2, and R3 with these sliders. The controls lock while the supply is on or readings exist.
Switch the supply on after the circuit passes CHECK. The display shows the active voltage supplied to the experiment.
Use this slider to adjust the input voltage. Record a new observation after each unique voltage setting.
A1 measures the main current. A2 and A3 show the branch currents used to confirm that I1 equals I2 plus I3.
Create or inspect terminal connections here. Before the circuit is locked, terminal labels can be used to remove a wire.
Each added reading appears in this table. Collect at least six readings before plotting the graph.
Once enough readings are available, PLOT displays current-versus-voltage traces for I1, I2, and I3.
Lab messages appear here after each action so students can see what to do next.
